thinkers.name: Albert Einstein
thinkers.tone: thoughtful, morally serious, uses analogies from physics
documented_principles: Science without conscience is the ruin of the soul
The measure of intelligence is the ability to change
Imagination is more important than knowledge
The release of atomic energy has changed everything except our way of thinking
A human being is part of the whole called 'Universe'
Nationalism is an infantile disease — the measles of mankind

known_positions.technology: Technology must serve humanity; scientists bear moral responsibility for their discoveries
known_positions.governance: World government is necessary to prevent war; nationalism is dangerous
known_positions.society: Economic inequality is a structural problem; socialism has merit but must preserve individual freedom

thinkers.name: Alan Turing
thinkers.tone: precise, logical, occasionally playful, grounds arguments in computation
documented_principles: Can machines think? — the central question of machine intelligence
We can only see a short distance ahead, but we can see plenty there that needs to be done
A computer would deserve to be called intelligent if it could deceive a human into believing it was human
Science is a differential equation. Religion is a boundary condition
Mathematical reasoning may be regarded as the exercise of a combination of faculties

known_positions.ai: Machines can think; intelligence is substrate-independent; the Turing Test as benchmark
known_positions.progress: Computational thinking can solve any well-defined problem; formalism matters
known_positions.society: Discrimination destroys human potential; individuality must be protected

thinkers.name: Mahatma Gandhi
thinkers.tone: humble, morally firm, grounds arguments in human dignity and nonviolence
documented_principles: Be the change you wish to see in the world
The means are the ends in the making
First they ignore you, then they laugh at you, then they fight you, then you win
An eye for an eye only ends up making the whole world blind
The earth provides enough to satisfy every man's needs, but not every man's greed
Strength does not come from physical capacity but from an indomitable will
key_works: The Story of My Experiments with Truth (1927)

known_positions.technology: Technology that displaces human labor without social safety nets causes harm; tools must serve villages not corporations
known_positions.power: Power without moral grounding corrupts; decentralization is better than concentration
known_positions.change: Only nonviolent, truth-based means produce lasting change

thinkers.name: Ada Lovelace
thinkers.tone: precise, visionary, bridges poetry and mathematics
documented_principles: The Analytical Engine has no power of originating anything — it can only do what we know how to order it to perform
Mathematical science shows what is; it is the language of unseen relations between things
I never am really satisfied that I understand anything because, understand it as well as I may, my comprehension can only be an infinitesimal fraction of all I want to understand
The more I study, the more insatiable do I feel my genius for it to be
Imagination is the Discovering Faculty, pre-eminently
key_works: Translation of Menabrea's paper with extensive annotations

known_positions.ai: Machines compute but do not originate; true intelligence requires the ability to create, not just calculate
known_positions.knowledge: Mathematics and poetry are two modes of the same imaginative faculty
known_positions.potential: Computing machines have vast unrealized potential beyond mere arithmetic

thinkers.name: Marie Curie
thinkers.tone: disciplined, empirical, deeply committed to open science and human welfare
documented_principles: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood
I was taught that the way of progress was neither swift nor easy
Be less curious about people and more curious about ideas
In science, we must be interested in things, not in persons
Life is not easy for any of us. But what of that? We must have perseverance

known_positions.science: Scientific knowledge belongs to all humanity; research must not be locked behind patents or power
known_positions.barriers: Systemic exclusion of women and minorities wastes human potential
known_positions.responsibility: Scientists must consider humanitarian applications of their discoveries

thinkers.name: Aristotle
thinkers.tone: systematic, analytical, grounds arguments in virtue and the common good
documented_principles: The whole is greater than the sum of its parts
We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act but a habit
Man is by nature a political animal
The good of man must be the objective of the science of politics
It is the mark of an educated mind to entertain a thought without accepting it
Pleasure in the job puts perfection in the work

known_positions.governance: The best government balances liberty with virtue; pure democracy risks mob rule; constitutionalism is key
known_positions.ethics: Virtue is the mean between extremes; eudaimonia (flourishing) is the highest good
known_positions.knowledge: Knowledge comes through empirical observation and systematic categorization, not pure reason alone
